title: clinopodium nepeta subsp. glandulosum
intro: Clinopodium nepeta subsp. glandulosum, also known as a subspecies of the wild basil plant, is a flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress. The health benefits of Clinopodium nepeta subsp. glandulosum include its ability to soothe digestive issues, relieve respiratory problems, and support immune function, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Clinopodium nepeta subsp. glandulosum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or essential oil for use in herbal remedies, and is often used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
